pick.rationale: Florida enters as an overwhelming favorite, ranking #4 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with an elite 26-7 record, top-10 offense, and top-5 defense. The efficiency gap is staggering, with Florida's +33.83 adjusted efficiency margin dwarfing Lehigh's -10.38, reflecting a massive talent and competition level disparity between the SEC champion and the Patriot League representative. While Lehigh earned their bid by winning the Patriot League Tournament and surviving a First Four matchup, the jump from mid-major competition to facing a national championship contender presents an almost insurmountable challenge.

reasoningStep.title: Florida vs Lehigh Round of 64 Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: Florida is a massive favorite as a #1 seed against 16-seed Lehigh, with elite rankings (#4 nationally), SEC championship form, and a 44-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage.
reasoningStep.evidence: Florida ranks #4 in KenPom/Torvik with +33.83 adj EM, elite top-10 offense and top-5 defense, won SEC championship with 111-77 dominant victory over Arkansas. Lehigh ranks #282-284 with -10.38 adj EM, 18-16 record, poor offense (289th-290th) and below-average defense (262nd), won Patriot League Tournament but required First Four victory. Historical 1/16 seed matchups heavily favor the 1-seed, and no injury concerns were identified for Florida.

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.

The adjusted efficiency margin gap is enormous: Florida +33.83 vs Lehigh -10.38, a difference of about 44 points.

Historically, 1-seeds beat 16-seeds overwhelmingly (until UMBC beat Virginia in 2018, it was 135-0). Even since then, 1-seeds still win the vast majority of these matchups.

There's no credible upset path for Lehigh here. They're over 40 spots lower in rankings, have a negative efficiency margin vs Florida's elite positive one, and face a massive talent and competition level gap.
